things.name: Masmak Fortress
things.description: 1865 mud-brick fort where Ibn Saud recaptured Riyadh in 1902, founding modern Saudi Arabia

things.name: National Museum of Saudi Arabia
things.description: Eight galleries covering 4.6 billion years of Saudi and Arabian history and heritage

things.name: Edge of the World (Jebel Fihrayn)
things.description: Dramatic 300m escarpment with views stretching to infinity over the Tuwayq plateau

things.name: Al-Turaif District, Diriyah
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage first capital of the Saudi state with mud-brick palace ruins

things.name: Kingdom Centre Tower Sky Bridge
things.description: 323m skyscraper with a glass sky bridge between the two leaning towers

things.name: Riyadh Zoo
things.description: One of the Middle East's finest zoos with Arabian wildlife and rare white tigers

things.name: Deera Square (Chop-Chop Square)
things.description: Old city centre square surrounded by the Grand Mosque, courts and the Deera souk

things.name: Al Faisaliah Tower Observation Deck
things.description: Riyadh's first skyscraper with a glass globe at the top offering 360-degree city views

things.name: Camel Market (Souq Al-Jamal)
things.description: Authentic camel trading market where thousands of camels are bought and sold weekly

things.name: Boulevard Riyadh City
things.description: Massive entertainment district with concerts, restaurants and the Riyadh Season events
